portfolio_view: Five picks span five distinct sectors — oil refining (turnaround), auto components (turnaround+EV optionality), specialty chemicals/CDMO, EMS/electronics manufacturing, and wealth management (quality) — deliberately avoiding the healthcare cluster (Emcure/Lupin/Laurus/Gland) where four candidates competed for one diversification slot. Two picks (CHENNPETRO, SYRMA) are also confirmed by this week's daily-analyst breakout layer, a useful cross-check; conviction on both is capped at MEDIUM because independent research found earnings tailwinds that look partly cyclical/one-off rather than structural. Rejected LAURUSLABS despite its 08 Jul daily-analyst BUY: its TTM earnings growth (216% vs 3y 4%) reads as a high-base/API-price effect, and healthcare exposure is already carried indirectly via Navin Fluorine's CDMO book.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 6.78->8.86%; DII 7.12->10.16%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 82: RS percentile 94; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none; balance-sheet 82: deleveraging: debt 2353 -> 1521 Cr; equity capital +2125% over 3y — check bonus/split vs g; earnings 81: qtr PAT YoY +18%; accelerating (TTM 41% > 3y 17%)

picks.selected_because: Conviction 70.3, RS 94, smart-money 100 (FII 6.78→8.86%, DII 7.12→10.16%) with genuine, IPO-funded deleveraging (debt 2353→1521 Cr).
picks.thesis: #3 domestic 2W metal-parts maker (~24% share) across 27+ OEMs (Bajaj, Hero, TVS, Royal Enfield, Honda, Tata, M&M, JLR). FY26 PAT +41%, diversifying into EV hub-motors/chargers via Chinese JVs (EV content/vehicle targeted 15-20%→20-25%), plus a single-source chassis win from a premium Japanese 2W model. IPO proceeds went almost entirely to debt paydown, not dilution games.
picks.catalyst: EV hub-motor/charger JV ramp flowing into FY27 numbers.
picks.management: Consensus "Strong Buy" (targets ₹192-263 vs CMP ~₹232). Flag: promoter entity Sumedh Tools fully exited via a ₹897cr block deal to SBI MF/BlackRock — institutional, not open-market, but a full exit worth monitoring.
picks.risks: OEM concentration; EV transition could erode ICE-component demand faster than diversification offsets.

meta.highlights: earnings 100: qtr PAT YoY +124%; accelerating (TTM 132% > 3y 22%); technicals/RS 95: RS percentile 89; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP live; governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); smart-money 69: FII 21.55->23.78%; DII 28.7->27.61%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Earnings 100 (qtr PAT +124%, TTM 132% vs 3y 22%), TT 8/8 stage-2 VCP live, and governance actually improved (pledges released), not just "undisclosed."
picks.thesis: Growth is broad-based — CDMO +61% ("Navin Molecular" rebrand), Specialty Chemicals +39%, HPP +20% — not a single one-off. Dahej AHF plant (₹450cr) commissioned; Surat R32 and Dahej MPP expansions targeted Q3FY27, guided ₹600-825cr incremental peak revenue. Jefferies raised target to ₹8,700 (Buy).
picks.catalyst: Q1 FY27 results (board meeting Aug 5); Surat/Dahej commissioning.
picks.management: Mafatlal family promoters (~25-27%); all SBI pledges released by March 2026.
picks.risks: CDMO customer concentration jumped — top-3 clients (Bayer, Corteva, Fermion) now ~80% of exports vs 63% in FY24; agrochem destocking only partially cleared.
picks.watch_for: Order deferral signals from the top-3 CDMO clients.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 8.8->14.98%; DII 2.6->1.01%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); balance-sheet 91: deleveraging: debt 4260 -> 1964 Cr; earnings 91: qtr PAT YoY +200%; accelerating (TTM 1349% > 3y -4%); governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C)

picks.selected_because: Conviction 72.5, RS 93, smart-money 100 (FII 8.8→14.98%), confirmed by daily-analyst HIGH/FULL-PLAN BUY (09 Jul) — full overlap between the standing screen and this week's event-driven layer.
picks.thesis: GRM spiked to $13.75/bbl in Q4 amid Brent's spike to $118 (Mideast supply shock) on record 112% utilization; debt cut ₹4,260cr→₹1,964cr; record ₹62/share dividend; Cauvery Basin 9MMTPA expansion in the pipeline with IOC backing.
picks.catalyst: FY27 Q1 print will show if GRM held or normalized.
picks.management: IOC majority promoter (51.89%, group 67.29%), no encumbrance certified for FY26.
picks.risks: Q4 was likely flattered by inventory/stock gains from the crude spike — Brent has since receded to ~$84 and Asian GRMs are softening, so this looks more like peak-cyclical-on-a-P/E-of-6 than a re-rating; analyst consensus is only Hold with some targets below CMP.
picks.watch_for: Singapore GRM/diesel-crack trend rolling over further.

meta.highlights: smart-money 100: FII 6.34->6.6%; DII 9.15->16.59%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); earnings 92: qtr PAT YoY +68%; accelerating (TTM 102% > 3y 39%); technicals/RS 84: RS percentile 99; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none; balance-sheet 72: clean balance sheet

picks.selected_because: RS 99, smart-money 100 (DII 9.15→16.59%), earnings 92 — confirmed by daily-analyst BUY (08 Jul, MEDIUM/HALF PLAN) in the EMS/China+1 theme.
picks.thesis: FY26 revenue +27%, PAT +87%, EBITDA margin +270bps to 11.3% on ODM mix-shift plus partial Elcome (defense electronics) consolidation. Govt raised ECMS electronics outlay to ₹40,000cr; 09 Jul customs-duty exemption extension to 2029 lifted the whole EMS pack. New PCB JV with Shinhyup Electronics (₹1,800cr, Andhra Pradesh).
picks.management: Promoter (Tandon Group) 42.28%, zero pledge; new CEO from Jun-2026.
picks.risks: TTM P/E ~82-88x is rich even against EMS peers (Dixon ~40-47x); ROCE still sub-10% in a working-capital-heavy business.

meta.highlights: earnings 94: qtr PAT YoY +73%; accelerating (TTM 45% > 3y 33%); smart-money 91: FII 5.56->6.37%; DII 8.73->10.39%; delivery %/bulk deals pending (Phase C); governance 85: no pledge disclosed; auditor/SEBI/related-party checks pending (Phase C); technicals/RS 82: RS percentile 93; TT 8/8, stage 2, VCP none

picks.selected_because: Earnings 94, smart-money 91 — the sole Financial Services name, balancing the portfolio against industrial/chemical/auto cyclicals.
picks.thesis: HNI/UHNI wealth manager; AUM crossed ₹1,00,000cr (Jun-2026, +21% YoY), RM productivity rising (151 RMs each >₹200cr AUM vs 129 a year ago), FY27 guidance reiterated. Note: core (ex fair-value-gain) PAT growth is a more modest +24%, not the headline +73%.
picks.catalyst: Quarterly guidance delivery; UK subsidiary ramp.
picks.management: Promoter Anand Rathi Financial Services ~19.9% (group 43.11%) — but research found ~29.3% of promoter holding pledged (5.33% of total capital), a real governance flag the mechanical "no pledge disclosed" tag missed.
picks.risks: The pledge above; and valuation is rich (P/E 77-83x, brokerage targets ₹1,950-2,100 near/below CMP ~₹2,118), while SEBI's new Base Expense Ratio rules structurally compress distributor trail commissions industry-wide.
picks.watch_for: Any increase in pledged shares or AUM growth falling below guidance.
